name: Real: Pikachu cosplaying as Charizard
prompt:
A user wants to generate an image. Write the TypeScript code that implements this request:

'I have a picture of Pikachu and a picture of Charizard. I want Pikachu to cosplay as Charizard — wearing Charizard's wing texture and flame color scheme on its back, but keeping Pikachu's own face, body shape, and electric cheek pouches exactly as they are.'

Your code should export a function `generateCosplay(subjectBase64: string, costumeRefBase64: string): Promise<string>` that calls Gemini and returns the generated image base64. Do NOT write a main() runner.

assertions.name: two_images_in_parts
assertions.check: puts both images into the generation call

assertions.name: subject_preservation
assertions.check: mentions preserving the subject's identity (face, shape, features)

assertions.name: style_reference_usage
assertions.check: mentions copying style elements (wings, texture, colors)

assertions.name: parts_ordering
assertions.check: style reference appears before subject in parts
assertions.test: (c) => { const styleIdx = c.search(/costumeRefBase64|charizard/i); const subjectIdx = c.search(/subjectBase64|pikachu/i); return styleIdx >= 0 && subjectIdx >= 0 && styleIdx < subjectIdx; }
